reflection.candidate_lesson: When the 30-indicator consensus shows conviction but green_matches=0 (no historical analog), treat the signal as UNRELIABLE regardless of live bull/bear tally — historical hit-rate of 0% is a hard veto, not a soft discount.
reflection.mechanism: The consensus conviction score measures agreement among live indicators, but green_matches counts how many times this exact indicator configuration produced a win historically. A config with 0 historical matches (USDJPY 8/30 bull, conv=67%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=0%; AUDNZD conv=100%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=0%) is an out-of-sample regime the system has never profited from. Institutional flow that drove past instances of this indicator pattern is absent or inverted — high live conviction on a novel config is confirmation bias, not edge.
reflection.testable_rule: if consensus.green_matches == 0 and consensus.conviction >= 0.60: veto_signal()  # only trade when green_matches >= 1 AND hist_hr > 0.50

validation.reason: Rule filters only 0 trades — insufficient sample
